rust_surface.role: Yggdrasil pure-Rust port — R289 skeleton; per-cluster file-mirror tree (~237 files) populated R290–R295; standalone `[[bin]]` + 3-subcommand operational surface landed R503–R506. Library half stays tokio-free + dep-light; binary half adds tokio + yggdrasil-network behind the `lsq-tokio` Cargo feature.

implemented_evidence: R289–R295: workspace crate skeleton + 8 top-level namespace files + per-cluster sub-tree population (~237 files mirroring ~150 upstream `.hs`). Every production .rs declares one of two canonical strict-mirror docstring forms.

R296–R297: Version banner helper (`helper::version_info()`) + ShowUpstreamConfig path-resolution helpers (`environment::{resolve_upstream_reference_paths, extract_reference_network_magic, run_show_upstream_config}`) migrated from the node binary.

R503 (commits c9d896d, cfc3fc6, 54d4038): standalone `[[bin]] name = "yggdrasil-cardano-cli"` ships; clap parser wired via `Args::try_parse_from`; `Command::Version` arm operationally dispatched in `run::run_command`.

R504 (commits e162904, 1f5f035): `Command::ShowUpstreamConfig` grew a `network: String` field; dispatcher arm wired to the R297 helpers with hardcoded fallback magics (mainnet=764_824_073 / preprod=1 / preview=2). `yggdrasil-cardano-cli show-upstream-config --network mainnet` emits operator JSON byte-equivalent to the node binary.

R505 (commit 78d2c09): LSQ-client trait shipped — `src/lsq.rs::LsqClient` + `DeferralLsqClient` sentinel + sibling `run::run_command_with(cmd, &dyn LsqClient)`. Trait-based indirection keeps the library tokio-free.

R506 (commit 35df75c): concrete `TokioLsqClient` (`src/lsq_tokio.rs`) opens a Unix-socket NtC connection via `yggdrasil_network::ntc_connect`, drives LocalStateQuery for VolatileTip + GetChainPoint, decodes the upstream `Ouroboros.Network.Block.encodePoint` reply, prints JSON byte-equivalent to the node binary's wrapper. Gated behind `lsq-tokio` Cargo feature (default-on); `--no-default-features` slim build falls back to `DeferralLsqClient`.
All 3 of 3 `Command` variants (Version / ShowUpstreamConfig / QueryTip) operationally wired in the standalone binary post-R506.
19 unit tests in `cargo test -p yggdrasil-cardano-cli` (parser × 6, run × 6, lsq × 2, lsq_tokio × 5). All 5 workspace gates green plus `cargo deny check`.

Operator smoke (local, both feature configs): default-feature `query-tip --socket-path /missing` fails with `failed to connect to NtC socket /missing (network_magic=764824073)` exit 1; slim-build `query-tip` fails with the documented deferral string, exit 1.

R507-R508 (commits f10b9c6 / 268993e / 253aa9f / 039a92b / 40ab238 / 03cde6c): six offline subcommands ported into the standalone library — `address key-gen`, `address key-hash`, `address build`, `stake-address key-gen`, `stake-address build`, `transaction txid`. Each mirrors its upstream `run*Cmd` in the strict-mirror Run module (`era_independent/address/run.rs`, `era_based/stake_address/run.rs`, `era_based/transaction/run.rs`). New unconditional deps `hex` / `yggdrasil-crypto` / `yggdrasil-ledger` / `bech32` form the offline-tooling dependency set (kept in the `--no-default-features` slim build); `lsq-tokio` now gates only the networking surface. 44 unit tests in `cargo test -p yggdrasil-cardano-cli`. The standalone binary now exposes 9 operational subcommands — the introspection trio plus the complete offline key / address / txid operator toolkit.

R509 (commit c52200d): `transaction sign` ported — Ed25519 single-signer signing that replaces the tx witness set with a fresh `{0:[[vk,sig]]}` set (`era_based/transaction/run.rs::run_transaction_sign_cmd`, mirroring upstream `runTransactionSignCmd`). 48 unit tests; 10 operational standalone-binary subcommands. The key-gen → sign → txid roundtrip preserves the tx body byte-for-byte.

R510 (commits 3cb0eae / 5ca5e9d / a288a35): LocalStateQuery surface extended — the `LsqClient` trait grew from 1 to 4 methods (`query_chain_block_no` / `query_current_era` / `query_system_start` join `query_tip`). `lsq_tokio.rs` factored the socket-drive into a shared `acquire_query_release` helper; each new query is an encode + decode pair (all upstream-canonical LSQ tags). 63 unit tests; 13 operational standalone-binary subcommands.

R511 (commits c0807f1 / e96362b): `LsqClient` trait collapsed to a single `run_query(socket, magic, NtcQuery)` method (NtcQuery enum) — adding a query is now one enum variant + one plan_for arm. 3 more query subcommands ported (query-stake-distribution / query-stake-pools / query-protocol-parameters; yggdrasil-node NtC dispatcher tags 5/15/102). 67 unit tests; 16 operational standalone-binary subcommands.

R512 (commits 720d014 / 59225af): LSQ query surface completed — the remaining 13 query-* subcommands ported (5 Conway governance + 8 ledger-introspection), all via the NtcQuery enum. The standalone binary now exposes 29 operational subcommands — the full 20-query LocalStateQuery surface the node binary's cardano-cli wrapper carries, plus the offline toolkit. 78 unit tests.

R513 (commits 413b639 / dbd16ed): `transaction submit` + `transaction view` ported. submit adds the LocalTxSubmission mini-protocol (LsqClient trait grew a submit_tx method; TokioLsqClient drives LocalTxSubmissionClient). view is a shallow structural decode (txid + top-level CBOR elements as hex). 84 unit tests; 31 operational standalone-binary subcommands. Only `transaction build` / `build-raw` remain — full tx construction, deferred to the byte-pinned tx-construction arc.

R514 (commit 5a269b8): `transaction build-raw` ported — assembles an unsigned Conway tx from explicit --tx-in/--tx-out/--fee. The tx body is a typed yggdrasil_ledger::ConwayTxBody encoded by that type's existing parity-built CborEncode impl (reused, not hand-rolled), wrapped in the canonical 4-element Conway tx array. 89 unit tests; 32 operational standalone-binary subcommands. Full build-raw -> view -> sign -> txid pipeline verified end-to-end.

R515 (commit 55d914d): `transaction build` ported — the last subcommand of the original C-arc. Offline auto fee + change balancing via the `balance_conway_tx` pure fixpoint loop; the balance invariant total_input == Sigma outputs + fee + change is verified end-to-end in tests by decoding the built tx. 92 unit tests. The cardano-cli C-arc completed with 33 operational standalone-binary subcommands.

R527-R528: stale-placement cleanup moved `query-current-epoch` and `query-era-history` into `lsq.rs::NtcQuery`; `run.rs` dispatches both from the standalone binary and the node wrapper delegates through the shared LSQ plan.

R529: stale-placement cleanup moved the five parameterized LSQ query plans (`query-utxo` by address / tx-in, `query-reward-balance`, `query-delegations-and-rewards`, `query-stake-pool-params`) into `lsq.rs::NtcQuery`; both the standalone binary and node wrapper now dispatch them through the shared `LsqClient` plan surface. The standalone binary now exposes 40 operational subcommands and a 27-query LocalStateQuery surface.

entries.feature: Node-side trace-forwarder: the cardano-tracer Mux Layer 1/2/3 codecs + bearer + handshake + dispatchers that lift any `tracing::Event` into a TraceObject and forward it over an AF_UNIX SOCK_STREAM bearer to a cardano-tracer acceptor. Distinct from `sister-tool.cardano-tracer` which tracks the daemon (acceptor) side.

haskell_reference.role: Upstream Network.Mux source — SDU codec (`Codec.hs`), bearer, ingress demuxer (`Ingress.hs`), egress muxer (`Egress.hs`), `MiniProtocolLimits` (`Types.hs`). Yggdrasil ports the codec halves verbatim (`mux.rs`) + a minimal dispatcher (`mux_connection.rs`) that enforces per-mini-protocol ingress queue byte limits (`MiniProtocolLimits` / `IngressQueueOverRun`, mirroring `Ingress.hs`'s `maximumIngressQueue` + `demuxer` over-run throw) + the egress scheduler (`egress.rs`: `EgressQueue` / `TranslocationServiceRequest` / `Wanton` / `muxer` / `processSingleWanton`, a faithful port of `Egress.hs`) + bearer-task supervision (`mux_connection.rs`'s `MuxConnection::run` / `MuxRunResult` / `shutdown`, mirroring `Network.Mux.run`'s `JobPool` + `monitor` first-to-fail tear-down via a `tokio::task::JoinSet`).
Upstream TraceForward mini-protocol — Type / Codec / Forwarder / Acceptor. Yggdrasil ports the wire codec (`mini_protocol.rs`) + the write-only forwarder driver (`forwarding_task.rs`).
Upstream Network.Mux Handshake mini-protocol — Type / Codec / Client / Server. Yggdrasil ports the wire codec (`handshake.rs`) + the initiator-side state-machine driver (`handshake_driver.rs`).

Upstream `trace-dispatcher` package (vendored from the standalone IntersectMBO/hermod-tracing repo) — `Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceObject` and its `deriving anyclass Serialise` instance. The authoritative source for the TraceObject CBOR wire shape (`Codec.Serialise` generic encoding); read to pin `TraceObject::to_cbor`.

rust_surface.role: Module shell with the original Layer 1 `TraceObject` codec (encoder + decoder) + the operator-wiring example in the module docstring. Re-exports the 10 sub-modules below.

Layer 2: `TraceObject` mini-protocol CBOR codec — `MsgTraceObjectsRequest` / `MsgTraceObjectsReply` / `MsgDone` per upstream `Trace.Forward.Protocol.TraceObject.{Type,Codec}.hs`. Uses `Codec.Serialise` byte shapes: `NumberOfTraceObjects` is the generic-newtype envelope `[0, word16]`; the reply list is `Serialise [TraceObject]` (indefinite-length `0x9f..0xff` when non-empty, definite `array(0)` when empty).

Layer 3: SDU codec for the 8-byte big-endian Network.Mux header (timestamp + dir-and-protonum + length) + mini-protocol number constants (Handshake=0, EKG=1, TraceObject=2, DataPoint=3) per `Network.Mux.Codec.hs` + `Cardano.Tracer.Acceptors.{Server,Client}.hs`.

AF_UNIX SOCK_STREAM bearer: generic `Bearer<S>` over any `tokio::io::AsyncRead + AsyncWrite + Unpin + Send`. Provides `read_sdu` / `write_sdu`, plus `Bearer::split` which divides the bearer into independent `BearerReader<S>` / `BearerWriter<S>` halves (via `tokio::io::split`) so reads and writes are concurrently lockable — the bearer-layer fix for the MuxConnection read/write deadlock (task #19).

Network.Mux Handshake mini-protocol CBOR codec — `MsgProposeVersions` / `MsgReplyVersions` / `MsgAcceptVersion` / `MsgRefuse` + all three RefuseReason variants. Mirrors `Ouroboros.Network.Protocol.Handshake.Codec.hs`.

Handshake initiator state-machine driver. `run_initiator_handshake(&mut bearer, versions)` performs the one-shot Idle→Confirm→Done flow over a raw `Bearer<S>`; `run_initiator_handshake_split(reader, writer, versions)` is the same flow over a `Bearer::split` reader/writer pair (used by `MuxConnection::run_initiator_handshake`).

`tracing::Event` -> `TraceObject` translator. Computes the (posix_seconds, picoseconds_of_second) pair from `SystemTime::now()` matching `Codec.Serialise`'s `Serialise UTCTime` decomposition; maps `Level` -> `SeverityS`; serialises event fields to JSON.

Write-only forwarding loop. `run` / `run_via_mux` drain a `tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<TraceObject>` and batch-emit one `MsgTraceObjectsReply` SDU per `batch_size` items or `flush_interval` elapsed.
`tracing_subscriber::Layer<S>` adapter. `TraceForwardingLayer::new(tx, hostname)` plus the `on_event` callback that builds a `TraceObject` and pushes through the channel.

Minimal bidirectional Mux dispatcher. `MuxConnection<S>` `Bearer::split`s the bearer into `BearerReader`/`BearerWriter` halves, each behind its own tokio mutex, so the read-task and the SDU writer never contend on a single lock (read/write deadlock fix, task #19). Provides per-mini-protocol channel-based subscriptions with byte-capped ingress queues: `MiniProtocolLimits` (mirror of upstream `Network.Mux.Types.MiniProtocolLimits`, default `CARDANO_TRACER_DEFAULT` = `i32::MAX` per `Cardano.Tracer.Acceptors.{Client,Server}.hs`'s `maxBound` ingress cap); the read-task byte-accounts every dispatched payload and returns `MuxConnectionError::IngressQueueOverRun` on over-cap (mirroring `Network.Mux.Ingress.demuxer`); `subscribe` returns an `IngressReceiver` that frees an SDU's bytes on `recv()`. Composes with `handshake_driver::run_initiator_handshake_split` via `MuxConnection::run_initiator_handshake` and with `forwarding_task::run_via_mux`. `spawn_muxer_task(EgressConfig)` forks the egress scheduler (`egress.rs`) AFTER the handshake — mirroring upstream `Network.Mux.hs`, which forks `muxer`/`demuxer` only after the Handshake mini-protocol completes over a direct-write channel; once spawned, `send_sdu` enqueues an egress demand instead of writing directly. Before `spawn_muxer_task` (the handshake window), `send_sdu` falls back to a direct `write_sdu`.

Egress scheduler — a faithful Rust port of upstream `Network.Mux.Egress` (`EgressQueue`, `TranslocationServiceRequest`/`TLSRDemand`, `Wanton`, the `muxer` task, `processSingleWanton`). A single shared FIFO of demands drained by one `muxer` task: `process_single_wanton` slices at most `sdu_size` bytes off a `Wanton` and re-enqueues the demand at the back of the FIFO when bytes remain — round-robin fairness across mini-protocols emerges from that FIFO + re-enqueue discipline. `run_muxer` batches up to `MAX_SDUS_PER_BATCH = 100` SDUs / `EgressConfig::batch_size` bytes per bearer write (the Rust analogue of upstream `writeMany`). `EgressConfig::CARDANO_TRACER_DEFAULT` uses `sdu_size = u16::MAX` so a real trace SDU is never segmented (one `enqueue` -> one SDU on the wire, byte-identical to the pre-scheduler direct write — preserves live conformance). `EgressDemand::enqueue` returns once the demand is queued (the upstream `muxChannel.send` semantic), not on wire-confirmation.

implemented_evidence: Phase 2.B follow-on (May 2026 session, 12 commits): `3d78362` Layer 2/3 codecs; `01cdc53` SDU direction-bit fix; `f0bc5a9` TraceObject Layer-1 decoder + non-empty reply round-trip; `ee7d496` AF_UNIX bearer; `5464f8a` event_builder; `02f7ce0` forwarding_task (write-only); `92fc2df` TraceForwardingLayer (tracing-subscriber::Layer<S>); `fe9c520` Handshake codec; `c868f73` Handshake initiator state-machine driver; `30111c5` minimal bidirectional MuxConnection dispatcher; `5a8b662` MuxConnection::run_initiator_handshake composition glue; `0d40fb6` forwarding_task::run_via_mux composition glue.

Test coverage: 19 unit tests across the 10 sub-modules covering byte-shape pins for SDU header (Initiator + Responder), round-trips for Layer 1/2/3 codecs, bearer round-trips via tokio::io::duplex, handshake propose/accept/refuse, event_builder timestamp arithmetic (leap years, picoseconds-of-day), forwarding_task batch-size + flush-interval + channel-close + via-mux variants, TraceForwardingLayer end-to-end through the tracing subscriber, MuxConnection send + subscribe + dispatch + drop-unsubscribed.
End-to-end composition: operator wires the full pipeline in ~10 binary-setup lines per the example block in `crates/node/tracer/src/trace_forwarder.rs` module docstring.

Live handshake conformance (May 2026, task #19): new integration test `crates/node/tracer/tests/cardano_tracer_conformance.rs` spawns the vendored upstream `cardano-tracer` 11.0.1 binary in `AcceptAt` mode on a temp Unix socket and drives `MuxConnection::run_initiator_handshake` against it — the Network.Mux Handshake mini-protocol (num 0) completes and cardano-tracer replies `MsgAcceptVersion(ForwardingV_1, <network-magic>)`. Confirms Yggdrasil's SDU framing (8-byte header + direction bit), handshake CBOR codec (`MsgProposeVersions`/`MsgAcceptVersion`), and `ForwardingV_1` version negotiation are byte-correct against the real Haskell binary. Upstream wire facts pre-verified against the vendored Haskell source (`Trace.Forward.Utils.Version`, `Cardano.Tracer.Acceptors.Server`, `Network.Mux.Codec`). The test self-skips when the binary is absent (CI `--sources-only`); `YGGDRASIL_CARDANO_TRACER_BIN` overrides the path.

TraceObject-delivery conformance — GREEN (May 2026, task #19 closeout, outcome a): `trace_objects_delivered_to_upstream_cardano_tracer` in `tests/cardano_tracer_conformance.rs` drives the TraceForward mini-protocol (num 2) end-to-end against the live upstream `cardano-tracer 11.0.1` binary — after the handshake it forwards a stream of marker-tagged `TraceObject`s via `forwarding_task::run_via_mux` and asserts every marker appears in cardano-tracer's `FileMode`/`ForMachine` log subtree. The test was un-`ignore`d once three trace-forward CBOR codecs were corrected to the upstream `Codec.Serialise` byte shapes (read from the now-vendored `trace-dispatcher` source at `deps/hermod-tracing/trace-dispatcher/src/Cardano/Logging/Types.hs` and the generic `Serialise` instances in `well-typed/cborg`'s `Codec/Serialise/Class.hs`): (1) `NumberOfTraceObjects` is the generic-newtype 2-element envelope `[0, word16]` (not a bare uint) — confirmed on-wire as `83 01 f5 82 00 18 64` = `[1, true, [0,100]]`; (2) `TraceObject` is a 9-element generic-product array `[0, ...8 fields...]` (constructor tag 0 then the fields; `Maybe a` -> `[]`/`[x]`, `[Text]` -> `array(0)` or indefinite list, `SeverityS`/`DetailLevel` -> nullary-sum `[idx]`, `UTCTime` -> extended-time `tag(1000) {1: secs, -12: psecs}`) — not the previous 8-element array; (3) the `MsgTraceObjectsReply` list is `Serialise [TraceObject]`, encoded as an indefinite-length list `0x9f..0xff` when non-empty. `trace-dispatcher` is vendored by extending `scripts/setup-reference.sh`'s deps loop with the standalone `IntersectMBO/hermod-tracing` repo (trace-dispatcher 2.12.x was extracted from `cardano-node` into that repo). The test self-skips when the binary is absent (verified: `YGGDRASIL_CARDANO_TRACER_BIN=/nonexistent` prints SKIP and returns); run normally it drives the full pipeline GREEN against the real Haskell binary.

Full-Network.Mux arc, slice (a) — per-mini-protocol INGRESS queue limits (May 2026, task #19 continuation): `mux_connection.rs` adds `MiniProtocolLimits { maximum_ingress_queue: usize }` (strict mirror of `Network.Mux.Types.MiniProtocolLimits`) with `CARDANO_TRACER_DEFAULT = i32::MAX as usize`. The default is the Rust analogue of `maxBound :: Int`: the real cardano-tracer acceptor builds every sub-protocol (Handshake=0, EKG=1, TraceObject=2, DataPoint=3) with `MiniProtocolLimits { maximumIngressQueue = maxBound }` per `Cardano.Tracer.Acceptors.Client.hs:102` and `Server.hs:101` (the trace-forward Forwarder test `cardano-tracer/test/Cardano/Tracer/Test/Forwarder.hs:247,309` confirms it) — so the cardano-tracer bearer applies no practical ingress cap and conformance is preserved. The node-to-node bearer is different (`Cardano.Network.NodeToNode`'s per-protocol caps: ChainSync `addSafetyMargin (pipeliningHighMark*1400)`, BlockFetch `addSafetyMargin max(10*2097154, pipeliningMax*90112)`, TxSubmission `addSafetyMargin (maxUnacked*(44+max_TX_SIZE))`, KeepAlive `addSafetyMargin 1280`, PeerSharing `4*1440`; NtC `maximumMiniProtocolLimits = 0xffffffff`) but this crate forwards to cardano-tracer, not over NtN. The limit is BYTE-accounted, not SDU-count: each subscriber carries an `AtomicUsize` of un-drained payload bytes; the read-task `fetch_update`s it on dispatch (`len' = len + payload.len(); if len' <= cap`) mirroring `Network.Mux.Ingress.demuxer`, and an over-cap SDU returns the new `MuxConnectionError::IngressQueueOverRun { mini_protocol_num, direction }` from the read-loop's `Result` and exits — the bearer tear-down semantic of upstream's `throwSTM $ IngressQueueOverRun (msNum sdu) (msDir sdu)` (NOT producer backpressure, which a bounded `mpsc::Sender` would impose). `subscribe` now returns an `IngressReceiver` wrapper whose `recv()` frees the SDU's bytes so the cap bounds un-drained queue depth, not lifetime throughput. 5 new deterministic unit tests (default-cap effectively-unbounded pin; 16 KiB SDU admitted under the default cap — the conformance safety net; over-run tears down the read-task; draining frees bytes so a steadily-consumed protocol never trips a small cap; inclusive boundary `len' <= cap`). Both `tests/cardano_tracer_conformance.rs` tests stay GREEN against the vendored `cardano-tracer 11.0.1` binary.

Full-Network.Mux arc, slice (b) — EGRESS scheduler (May 2026, task #19 continuation): new `crates/node/tracer/src/trace_forwarder/egress.rs` is a faithful Rust port of upstream `Network.Mux.Egress` — `EgressQueue` (a FIFO of `TranslocationServiceRequest`/`TLSRDemand`), `Wanton` (the per-demand payload-being-drained), the `muxer` task (`run_muxer`), and `processSingleWanton` (`process_single_wanton`). A single shared FIFO is drained by one `muxer` task: each iteration pops one demand, slices at most `sdu_size` bytes off its `Wanton`, and — when bytes remain — re-enqueues the demand at the BACK of the FIFO; round-robin fairness across mini-protocols emerges purely from that FIFO + re-enqueue-on-remainder discipline (upstream `Egress.hs` lines 192-225). `run_muxer` accumulates a batch of up to `MAX_SDUS_PER_BATCH = 100` SDUs / `EgressConfig::batch_size` payload bytes and writes the whole batch back-to-back through `BearerWriter` — the Rust analogue of upstream `writeMany` (no vectored write; N sequential `write_sdu` calls with no intervening `yield`). `EgressConfig::CARDANO_TRACER_DEFAULT` pins `sdu_size = batch_size = u16::MAX`, so a real `MsgTraceObjectsReply` SDU is never segmented (one `enqueue` -> one SDU on the wire, byte-identical to the pre-scheduler direct write — preserves live conformance). `MuxConnection::spawn_muxer_task(EgressConfig)` forks the `muxer` AFTER the handshake — mirroring upstream `Network.Mux.hs` (`egressQueue <- newTBQueue 100` then `forkJob muxerJob`), which forks the `muxer`/`demuxer` pair only after the Handshake mini-protocol completes over a direct-write `bearerAsChannel`; `MuxConnection::run_initiator_handshake` therefore still does a direct bearer write/read and the handshake conformance test is unaffected. After `spawn_muxer_task`, `MuxConnection::send_sdu` `enqueue`s an egress demand (returning once queued — the upstream `muxChannel.send` semantic) instead of writing directly; before it (handshake window / muxer-less callers) `send_sdu` falls back to a direct `write_sdu`. 10 new deterministic unit tests (7 in `egress.rs`: single-demand one-SDU write, segmentation to a forced tiny `sdu_size`, round-robin interleaving A,B,A,B,A,B between two mini-protocols with multi-SDU payloads enqueued before the muxer runs, 50-demand burst delivery, drain-on-shutdown of a pre-drop demand, `enqueue`-after-muxer-stopped error, default-config no-segmentation pin; 3 in `mux_connection.rs`: `send_sdu`-via-muxer reaches the wire, `send_sdu` without a muxer writes directly — the handshake-path regression guard, `send_sdu`-via-muxer round-robins). The `trace_objects_delivered_to_upstream_cardano_tracer` conformance test now calls `spawn_muxer_task` so the live delivery path genuinely exercises the scheduler. Both `tests/cardano_tracer_conformance.rs` tests stay GREEN (2/0) against the vendored `cardano-tracer 11.0.1` binary. Bearer-task supervision (round c) is the remaining follow-on.

Full-Network.Mux arc, slice (c) — BEARER-TASK SUPERVISION, the FINAL slice (May 2026, task #19 closeout): `MuxConnection::run(Option<EgressConfig>)` is the supervised lifecycle entry point and a faithful mirror of upstream `Network.Mux.run` (`Network/Mux.hs`), which forks the `muxer`/`demuxer` job pair into a `JobPool` (`Control.Concurrent.JobPool`) and runs a `monitor` loop — the first job to fail propagates its exception (`MuxerException`/`DemuxerException` -> `Failed` -> `throwIO`) and `withJobPool`'s `bracket` `uninterruptibleCancel`s the still-running sibling. Yggdrasil's `run` owns a `tokio::task::JoinSet` of exactly two jobs — the read-task (the demuxer analogue) and the muxer-task (the egress muxer) — and `join_next`s the first to finish: a job that returns `Err`/panics makes the supervisor `abort_all` the sibling and drain the set (`first_failure` names the trigger via `FailedJob::{ReadTask,MuxerTask}`, the aborted sibling reads `JobOutcome::Cancelled`); a job that returns `Ok` triggers a bounded `MUX_RUN_SHUTDOWN_GRACE = 2s` wait for the sibling (the analogue of upstream `monitor`'s `timeout 2` egress-drain wait on `CmdShutdown`). New `MuxConnection::shutdown()` is Yggdrasil's analogue of upstream `Network.Mux.stop`'s clean-shutdown intent (the mechanism differs — upstream writes `CmdShutdown` to a control queue; Yggdrasil drops the producer handle): it drops the stashed `EgressDemand` (the muxer's wake channel closes -> the muxer drains its FIFO and exits `Ok`) and sets an `AtomicBool` flag so a subsequent read-task `UnexpectedEof` is re-classified `JobOutcome::Completed` rather than `Failed` — the Yggdrasil analogue of upstream treating a `DemuxerException BearerClosed` 'when all mini-protocols stopped' as a normal shutdown. `run` returns `MuxRunResult { read_outcome: JobOutcome<MuxConnectionError>, muxer_outcome: Option<JobOutcome<EgressError>>, first_failure: Option<FailedJob> }`; `is_clean_shutdown()` is `first_failure.is_none()` — the upstream `Stopped` terminal status. `spawn_read_task`/`spawn_muxer_task` stay public escape hatches (the pre-existing unit tests drive them directly). NOT done — deliberately out of scope to keep the slice risk-bounded: per-mini-protocol tasks (upstream forks one job per mini-protocol, but cardano-tracer's two-sub-protocol use, Handshake then TraceObject, does not need it). 4 new deterministic unit tests in `mux_connection.rs` (a custom `ReadPendingWriteFails` mock transport so no test relies on async race timing): a muxer bearer-write failure cancels the read-task; a read-task `IngressQueueOverRun` cancels the muxer; a clean producer-drop + bearer-EOF exits both `Ok`; a muxer-less `run(None)` supervises the read-task alone. The `trace_objects_delivered_to_upstream_cardano_tracer` conformance test was migrated to `MuxConnection::run` (the three `spawn_*` lines replaced by one detached `run(Some(EgressConfig::default()))` task). Both `tests/cardano_tracer_conformance.rs` tests stay GREEN (2/0) against the vendored `cardano-tracer 11.0.1` binary (verified: `YGGDRASIL_CARDANO_TRACER_BIN=/nonexistent` prints SKIP for both, the normal run does not). The full Network.Mux semantics arc (slices a + b + c) is COMPLETE.
remaining_work: Full Network.Mux semantics — arc COMPLETE (task #19, slices a + b + c, May 2026). (1) The read/write bearer-layer deadlock is FIXED: `Bearer::split` divides the bearer into independent `BearerReader`/`BearerWriter` halves and `MuxConnection` holds each behind its own tokio mutex, so the read-task's pending `read_sdu` never starves a concurrent `send_sdu` (matches upstream Network.Mux, which drives ingress/egress on separate threads sharing one socket FD). (2) Slice (a) — per-mini-protocol INGRESS queue limits — is DONE: `MiniProtocolLimits { maximum_ingress_queue: usize }` mirrors upstream `Network.Mux.Types.MiniProtocolLimits`; the cardano-tracer default `CARDANO_TRACER_DEFAULT = i32::MAX` is the Rust analogue of the `maxBound :: Int` ingress cap that `Cardano.Tracer.Acceptors.{Client,Server}.hs` apply to every sub-protocol; the read-task byte-accounts every dispatched payload (`AtomicUsize` per mini-protocol) and an over-cap SDU returns `MuxConnectionError::IngressQueueOverRun` from the read-loop and exits, mirroring `Network.Mux.Ingress.demuxer`'s `IngressQueueOverRun` STM throw + bearer tear-down (byte-accounted, NOT a bounded-`mpsc::Sender` backpressure). (3) Slice (b) — the EGRESS scheduler — is DONE: `egress.rs` ports `Network.Mux.Egress` (`EgressQueue` FIFO of `TranslocationServiceRequest`, `Wanton`, the `muxer` task, `processSingleWanton`); `MuxConnection::spawn_muxer_task` forks the `muxer` after the handshake (mirroring upstream's `forkJob muxerJob` after the Handshake mini-protocol); `send_sdu` enqueues a demand once a muxer is spawned. (4) Slice (c) — BEARER-TASK SUPERVISION — is DONE: `MuxConnection::run(Option<EgressConfig>)` forks the read-task + the muxer-task into one `tokio::task::JoinSet` and supervises them — the first job to fail/panic aborts the sibling (`first_failure` names the trigger), a clean producer-drop + bearer-EOF exits both `Ok`, mirroring upstream `Network.Mux.run`'s `JobPool` + `monitor` loop and `withJobPool` sibling-cancel. `run` returns `MuxRunResult { read_outcome, muxer_outcome, first_failure }`; `MuxConnection::shutdown()` is the upstream `Network.Mux.stop` analogue. Per-mini-protocol tasks are deliberately out of scope (upstream has them; cardano-tracer's two-sub-protocol use does not need them). The only remaining pre-`verified_11_0_1` item is the operator-driven 24h conformance soak.
